seoTitle: Fix Instagram Live Not Working or Not Eligible (2026)
metaDescription: Instagram Live missing or showing "Not Eligible"? Learn why it happens — account age, strikes, region limits — and the steps to get Live working again.
intro: If the Live option has vanished from your Instagram camera or throws a "Not Eligible" message, it's usually tied to your account's age, standing, or region rather than a random glitch. This guide walks through why Instagram restricts Live access and how to check your eligibility so you can start streaming again. By the end you'll know which settings and account details to verify before trying to go live.

steps.heading: Understand why Instagram disables Live
steps.body: Instagram can hide or block the Live feature because of account age requirements, community guideline strikes, regional restrictions, or an outdated app version. Knowing which of these applies to you narrows down the fix.

steps.heading: Check your eligibility and account status
steps.body: Look at your account age, whether you've received any recent guideline warnings, and confirm your region supports Live. These factors together determine whether the option appears at all.

steps.heading: Fix app and account settings
steps.body: Update the Instagram app to the latest version and review your account settings for anything flagged as a restriction. An outdated app is one of the more common causes of Live simply not showing up.

steps.heading: Refresh the app to restore the feature
steps.body: After adjusting settings, close and reopen the app (or log out and back in) so Instagram re-evaluates your eligibility and the Live option reappears.

steps.heading: Do final checks before going live
steps.body: Confirm the Live icon is now visible and test starting a stream briefly to make sure there are no lingering errors before relying on it for a real broadcast.

faq.question: Why does Instagram say I'm not eligible for Live?
faq.answer: This typically happens due to account age requirements, a recent community guideline strike, regional limitations, or running an outdated version of the app.

faq.question: Can a new Instagram account use Live right away?
faq.answer: New or very recently created accounts often face temporary restrictions on Live, so age requirements are one of the first things to check.

faq.question: Will updating the app actually bring back Live?
faq.answer: In many cases yes — an outdated app version is a common reason the Live option disappears, and updating followed by a refresh can restore it.

faq.question: How long do Live restrictions from guideline strikes last?
faq.answer: The length varies by case, but reviewing your account for recent violations and resolving them is the main way to work toward restoring access.

faq.question: Is it safe to try these fixes myself?
faq.answer: Yes, checking eligibility, updating the app, and refreshing your settings are all safe, standard troubleshooting steps that don't risk your account.
